text:
Unit tests check individual parts of your code, like a function or a method. They help you find problems early. Integration tests, on the other hand, check how different parts of the system work together. They catch issues that might only appear when components interact.

Many developers use test-driven development, or TDD. In TDD, you write a test before you write the code. The test fails at first. Then you write just enough code to make it pass. This approach gives you high confidence that your code works correctly. When you run all your tests regularly, you can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking old functionality. Testing provides security and saves time.
